title: download.sh checksum branch only recognizes arm64, fails on other non-x86 hosts
body: download.sh checks `if [ "$CPU_ARCH" = "arm64" ]` to decide between 'md5' and 'md5sum -c', but on some Linux aarch64 boxes 'uname -m' reports 'aarch64' rather than 'arm64', so the script falls through to the macOS-style md5 command and fails.

title: Chat dialogs exceeding max_seq_len are truncated without warning
body: Llama.chat_completion (built on generation.py) appears to silently drop or truncate the oldest turns when the encoded dialog plus system prompt exceeds max_seq_len, instead of raising or logging that truncation happened, which can silently change answers.

title: Document expected SentencePiece model format for Tokenizer
body: Tokenizer.__init__ just asserts os.path.isfile(model_path) and loads it with SentencePieceProcessor, but there's no note on what vocab size or special-token layout (bos/eos/pad ids) is expected, which matters if someone wants to swap in a custom tokenizer.model.

title: Llama.build() assumes CUDA is available
body: Llama.build() calls initialize_model_parallel and moves state to the GPU without checking torch.cuda.is_available() first, so trying to load a small checkpoint on a CPU-only box for debugging fails with a low-level CUDA error instead of a clear message.

title: example_chat_completion.py prints UNSAFE_ERROR text as if it were a normal reply
body: When the model or the special-tag guard trips UNSAFE_ERROR inside generation.py, example_chat_completion.py just prints whatever comes back without checking for that sentinel string, so it's indistinguishable from a real assistant response in the demo output.
